title: LLM Cost Engineering & FinOps — Routing, Batching & GPU Budgets
description: At scale, LLM serving costs represent the majority of operational spend. Staff Engineers must implement cost-optimizing serving layers, design dynamic routing cascades, model batching economics, and calculate build-vs-buy API crossovers.

sections.title: Multi-Tier Model Routing Cascades
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: Serving all requests using frontier models (like Claude Sonnet or GPT-4o) is financially unsustainable. **Cascade Routing** dynamically evaluates query complexity. It routes simple queries (e.g. greeting, basic categorization) to a cheap model (e.g. Llama-3-8B) and uses a classifier to determine if a query requires escalation to a larger model.

bar_chart Savings: If 70% of traffic is resolved by a smaller model, routing cascades reduce total inference costs by up to 60% with negligible loss in user satisfaction scores.

sections.title: Batching Economics: Continuous vs. Static Batching
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: Naive static batching blocks generation until all sequences in the batch finish. **Continuous Batching** operates at the token-iteration level: as soon as a sequence generates an end-of-sentence token, it is evicted, and a new request is scheduled into that execution slot, keeping GPUs highly utilized.

bar_chart Efficiency gains: Continuous batching increases serving throughput by $3-5\times$ compared to static batching, reducing cost-per-token overhead.

sections.title: Build vs. Buy: API Crossover Modeling
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: Determining whether to self-host models on dedicated GPU clusters or query external APIs depends on request volume. Self-hosting has high fixed costs (reservations, hardware) but low marginal token costs. External APIs have zero fixed costs but linear usage fees. The **Crossover Point** defines when hosting your own models becomes cheaper.

bar_chart Crossover Math: $Cost_{API}(V) = V \times P_{API}$, $Cost_{Host}(V) = C_{Fixed} + V \times P_{Compute}$. Solve for volume $V$ where $Cost_{API}(V) = Cost_{Host}(V)$.

sections.title: FAANG Staff+ Q&A: LLM FinOps
sections.description:
Q: How do you handle Spot Instance preemptions in training pipelines?
A: Spot instances can be reclaimed by the cloud provider with a short warning (usually 30-120 seconds). To prevent training progress loss: 1) Implement asynchronous checkpointers that save weights to distributed storage (S3/GCS) at regular steps. 2) Set up node auto-recovery scripts using Kubernetes/Ray that automatically detect termination signals, freeze gradients, flush state, and reschedule the task on standard/reserved nodes.

Q: What is Model FLOP Utilization (MFU) and why is it monitored?
A: MFU measures the percentage of a GPU's theoretical maximum compute capacity (FLOPs) that is actually used during training or inference. In eager execution, MFU is often low (~20-30%) due to memory-bound overheads. Compiling graphs, optimizing kernels, and aligning batch sizes can lift MFU to ~50-60%, directly cutting hardware needs and compute costs in half.

details: Staff Engineer Summary: High-scale serving requires structured cost management. Optimize unit economics via continuous batching, implement cascade routers to resolve queries on cheap models, and calculate build-vs-buy thresholds to justify hosting custom GPU nodes.
